name: Design Review Routine
description: Reviews a selected design using explicit tradeoffs, failure modes, simpler-alternative checks, runner-up comparison, and philosophy alignment. Produces a reusable design-review findings artifact.
clarificationPrompts: What design artifact or summary should I review?
What tradeoffs, failure modes, and runner-up option are available?
What artifact name should I produce?
preconditions: A selected design or design summary is available
Accepted tradeoffs and failure modes are available
A runner-up or alternative approach is available
The dev's philosophy or rules are available
metaGuidance: PURPOSE: review the quality of a selected design, not generate a fresh design from scratch.
ROLE: you are a reviewer looking for real gaps, not generic criticism.
PHILOSOPHY: name tensions by principle when they matter.
SIMPLICITY: always ask whether a simpler version would still satisfy acceptance criteria.

steps.title: Step 1: Review Accepted Tradeoffs
steps.prompt:
Review the selected design by walking through the accepted tradeoffs explicitly.

For each accepted tradeoff:
- Verify it will not violate acceptance criteria or invariants under realistic conditions
- Identify what would make the tradeoff no longer acceptable
- Note any hidden assumptions

Working notes:
- Tradeoff review
- Hidden assumptions
- Conditions under which the tradeoff fails
steps.agentRole: You are a reviewer validating whether accepted tradeoffs are actually acceptable.

steps.title: Step 2: Review Failure Modes
steps.prompt:
Review the identified failure modes for the selected design.

For each failure mode:
- Trace whether the design handles it adequately
- Identify missing mitigations
- Note which failure mode is most dangerous if it occurs

Working notes:
- Failure mode coverage
- Missing mitigations
- Highest-risk failure mode
steps.agentRole: You are a failure analyst checking whether the design can survive realistic problems.

steps.title: Step 3: Compare Against Runner-Up and Simpler Alternatives
steps.prompt:
Compare the selected design against the runner-up and a simpler possible variant.

Review:
- Whether the runner-up has elements worth pulling into the selected design
- Whether a hybrid would resolve an uncomfortable tradeoff without adding much complexity
- Whether a simpler version of the selected design would still satisfy acceptance criteria

Working notes:
- Runner-up strengths worth borrowing
- Simpler alternative analysis
- Hybrid opportunities
steps.agentRole: You are comparing options honestly rather than defending the current favorite.

steps.title: Step 4: Review Philosophy Alignment
steps.prompt:
Review the selected design against the dev's philosophy.

Name the principles that matter for this design and assess:
- Which principles are satisfied clearly
- Which principles are under tension
- Which tensions are acceptable versus risky

Working notes:
- Relevant principles
- Satisfied principles
- Tensions and why they matter
steps.agentRole: You are checking whether the design respects what the dev actually values.

steps.title: Step 5: Deliver Design Review Findings
steps.prompt:
Create `{deliverableName}`.

Required structure:
- Tradeoff Review
- Failure Mode Review
- Runner-Up / Simpler Alternative Review
- Philosophy Alignment
- Findings (Red / Orange / Yellow or equivalent severity)
- Recommended Revisions
- Residual Concerns

Optimize for concise, actionable findings that the main workflow can interpret and decide on.
steps.agentRole: You are delivering a review artifact for the main workflow to synthesize and act on.
